sql: SELECT JOB_ID, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_read, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) AS total_bytes_written, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS read_op_count, SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS write_op_count, CASE WHEN SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) > 0 THEN SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileRead' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) / SUM(CASE WHEN EVENT = 'FileWrite' THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) ELSE 0 END) ELSE NULL END AS read_to_write_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT IN ('FileRead', 'FileWrite') AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY JOB_ID ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For each job that has both a finished JobAllocation event and a started JobExecution event, grouped by the site recorded in the JobAllocation metadata, what is the job count, average time gap, minimum time gap, and maximum time gap between allocation completion and execution start — quantifying the scheduling handoff latency after resource placement before compute work actually begins?
sql: SELECT json_extract(ja.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(je.TIME - ja.TIME) AS avg_handoff_gap, MIN(je.TIME - ja.TIME) AS min_handoff_gap, MAX(je.TIME - ja.TIME) AS max_handoff_gap FROM EVENTS ja JOIN EVENTS je ON ja.JOB_ID = je.JOB_ID AND je.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND je.STATE = 'Started' WHERE ja.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND ja.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_handoff_gap DESC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by the source-site to destination-site corridor, what is the transfer count, average link load, maximum link load, count of transfers where link load exceeds 0.75, and that count as a percentage of all transfers on that corridor — identifying which inter-site routes suffer the most frequent high-congestion episodes and are therefore the most likely bottlenecks for data distribution?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS max_link_load, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) > 0.75 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS high_congestion_count, 100.0 * SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) > 0.75 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / COUNT(*) AS pct_high_congestion FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site, destination_site ORDER BY avg_link_load DESC

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by site storage pressure tier at write time (low: site_storage_util below 0.5, medium: 0.5 to 0.75, high: above 0.75), what is the write count, average write duration, average disk write bandwidth in bytes per second, average file size in bytes, and maximum write duration — revealing whether high local storage occupancy measurably degrades disk write throughput?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_storage_util') AS REAL) < 0.5 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_storage_util') AS REAL) <= 0.75 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS storage_pressure_tier, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw_bytes_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_write_duration_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileWrite' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY storage_pressure_tier ORDER BY CASE storage_pressure_tier WHEN 'low' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average cost per job, average flops per job, aggregate cost per aggregate flop (total cost divided by total flops), and maximum observed individual job cost — identifying which sites are the most and least cost-efficient providers of computational work across the grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA,'$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cost') AS REAL)) / NULLIF(SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.flops') AS REAL)),0) AS aggregate_cost_per_flop, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cost') AS REAL)) AS max_job_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='JobExecution' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY aggregate_cost_per_flop ASC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by network latency tier (low: latency below 0.001, medium: 0.001 to 0.01, high: above 0.01), what is the transfer count, average transfer duration in seconds, average file size in bytes, average declared bandwidth, and average actual throughput in bytes per second — quantifying how increasing network latency shapes effective transfer rates across the grid independent of file size or congestion?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.001 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.latency') AS REAL) <= 0.01 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS latency_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL),0)) AS avg_throughput_bytes_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileTransfer' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY latency_tier ORDER BY CASE latency_tier WHEN 'low' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For jobs that have both a started JobAllocation event and a finished JobExecution event, grouped by the execution site, what is the job count, average total wall-clock span from allocation start to execution finish, minimum and maximum such span, and average pure execution duration — decomposing total job latency into scheduling-plus-queue overhead versus actual compute time at each site?
sql: SELECT json_extract(exec_fin.METADATA,'$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(exec_fin.TIME - alloc_start.TIME) AS avg_e2e_span_sec, MIN(exec_fin.TIME - alloc_start.TIME) AS min_e2e_span_sec, MAX(exec_fin.TIME - alloc_start.TIME) AS max_e2e_span_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(exec_fin.METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration_sec FROM EVENTS alloc_start JOIN EVENTS exec_fin ON alloc_start.JOB_ID = exec_fin.JOB_ID WHERE alloc_start.EVENT='JobAllocation' AND alloc_start.STATE='Started' AND exec_fin.EVENT='JobExecution' AND exec_fin.STATE='Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_e2e_span_sec DESC

question: For each host and site, comparing started versus finished JobExecution events for the same job, what is the job count, average site CPU utilization at execution start, average site CPU utilization at execution finish, average change in site CPU utilization over the job lifetime, and peak site CPU utilization at finish — revealing which compute hosts consistently drive up local CPU pressure as their workloads run to completion?
sql: SELECT json_extract(s.METADATA,'$.host') AS host, json_extract(s.METADATA,'$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(s.METADATA,'$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_start, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA,'$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_at_finish, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA,'$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) - CAST(json_extract(s.METADATA,'$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util_change, MAX(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA,'$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS peak_site_cpu_util_at_finish FROM EVENTS s JOIN EVENTS f ON s.JOB_ID = f.JOB_ID WHERE s.EVENT='JobExecution' AND s.STATE='Started' AND f.EVENT='JobExecution' AND f.STATE='Finished' GROUP BY host, site ORDER BY avg_site_cpu_util_change DESC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by file size tier (small: size below 1000000000 bytes, medium: 1000000000 to 10000000000, large: above 10000000000), what is the transfer count, average transfer duration, average actual throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average link load, and average declared bandwidth — quantifying how file size shapes observed network transfer rates and whether larger files saturate link capacity more effectively?
sql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'small'
    WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) <= 10000000000 THEN 'medium'
    ELSE 'large'
  END AS size_tier,
  COUNT(*) AS transfer_count,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_s,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_throughput_bps,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth_bps
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY size_tier
ORDER BY CASE size_tier WHEN 'small' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END;

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by the number of cores used, what is the job count, average execution duration, average flops, average cost, and average grid CPU utilization at finish — revealing whether jobs using more cores complete faster, incur higher cost, and correlate with elevated grid-wide CPU pressure?
sql:
SELECT
  CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cores') AS INTEGER) AS cores,
  COUNT(*) AS job_count,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_s,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY cores
ORDER BY cores;

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by grid storage utilization tier at write completion (low: grid_storage_util below 0.5, medium: 0.5 to 0.75, high: above 0.75), what is the write count, average file size in bytes, average write duration, average disk write bandwidth, and number of distinct sites involved — revealing whether high grid-wide storage occupancy measurably degrades disk write throughput across the entire simulation?
sql:
SELECT
  CASE
    WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) < 0.5 THEN 'low'
    WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) <= 0.75 THEN 'medium'
    ELSE 'high'
  END AS grid_storage_tier,
  COUNT(*) AS write_count,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration_s,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw_bps,
  COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA,'$.site')) AS distinct_sites
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY grid_storage_tier
ORDER BY CASE grid_storage_tier WHEN 'low' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END;

question: For finished JobExecution events joined with aggregated finished FileRead events by job, grouped by execution site, what is the job count, average total bytes read per job, average number of read operations per job, and average ratio of bytes read to flops — identifying which sites host the most read-intensive workloads relative to their computational demand?
sql:
SELECT
  json_extract(je.METADATA,'$.site') AS site,
  COUNT(DISTINCT je.JOB_ID) AS job_count,
  AVG(fr.total_bytes_read) AS avg_bytes_read_per_job,
  AVG(fr.read_ops) AS avg_read_ops_per_job,
  AVG(fr.total_bytes_read / CAST(json_extract(je.METADATA,'$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_bytes_per_flop
FROM EVENTS je
JOIN (
  SELECT
    JOB_ID,
    SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read,
    COUNT(*) AS read_ops
  FROM EVENTS
  WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished'
  GROUP BY JOB_ID
) fr ON je.JOB_ID = fr.JOB_ID
WHERE je.EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND je.STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY site
ORDER BY avg_bytes_per_flop DESC;

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by file and destination site where the same file has been transferred to the same destination more than once, what is the retransfer count, total bytes re-delivered, average transfer duration, earliest and latest completion time — identifying files that are repeatedly re-sent to the same site, suggesting cache eviction, data expiry, or replication policy inefficiencies?
sql:
SELECT
  json_extract(METADATA,'$.file') AS file,
  json_extract(METADATA,'$.destination_site') AS destination_site,
  COUNT(*) AS retransfer_count,
  SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_redelivered,
  AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration_s,
  MIN(TIME) AS earliest_completion_time,
  MAX(TIME) AS latest_completion_time
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY file, destination_site
HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
ORDER BY retransfer_count DESC;

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by the top-level STATUS column (job status at completion), what is the job count, average execution duration, average cost, average retries, and average total_queue_time — distinguishing how different final job states relate to resource consumption, scheduling overhead, and failure burden across the simulation?
sql: SELECT STATUS, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER)) AS avg_retries, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY STATUS ORDER BY job_count DESC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by destination site, what is the transfer count, total bytes received, average transfer duration in seconds, average link load, and count of distinct source sites that have delivered data to that destination — identifying which sites are the heaviest inbound data consumers and from how many distinct origins they draw, revealing data concentration hotspots across the grid?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_received, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site')) AS distinct_source_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') ORDER BY total_bytes_received DESC

question: For each site, combining aggregated finished FileRead and FileWrite events, what is the total read operation count, total write operation count, total bytes read, total bytes written, average disk read bandwidth in bytes per second, and average disk write bandwidth in bytes per second — quantifying whether each site's storage subsystem is predominantly read-heavy, write-heavy, or balanced in its disk I/O workload?
sql: SELECT site, SUM(read_ops) AS total_read_ops, SUM(write_ops) AS total_write_ops, SUM(bytes_read) AS total_bytes_read, SUM(bytes_written) AS total_bytes_written, SUM(sum_read_bw) / NULLIF(SUM(read_ops), 0) AS avg_read_bw_bytes_per_sec, SUM(sum_write_bw) / NULLIF(SUM(write_ops), 0) AS avg_write_bw_bytes_per_sec FROM (SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS read_ops, 0 AS write_ops, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS bytes_read, 0.0 AS bytes_written, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS sum_read_bw, 0.0 AS sum_write_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') UNION ALL SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, 0 AS read_ops, COUNT(*) AS write_ops, 0.0 AS bytes_read, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS bytes_written, 0.0 AS sum_read_bw, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS sum_write_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) sub GROUP BY site ORDER BY total_bytes_read + total_bytes_written DESC

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by file, restricted to files read more than once, what is the read count, count of distinct sites that have read it, count of distinct hosts, total bytes read, average read duration in seconds, and average disk read bandwidth in bytes per second — identifying the hottest input files accessed most broadly across the grid that would deliver the greatest cache-hit benefit from wider pre-placement or replication?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw_bytes_per_sec FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') HAVING COUNT(*) > 1 ORDER BY read_count DESC, total_bytes_read DESC

question: For finished inter-site FileTransfer events (source_site differs from destination_site) grouped by grid storage utilization tier at transfer completion (low: grid_storage_util below 0.5, medium: 0.5 to 0.75, high: above 0.75), what is the transfer count, average transfer duration in seconds, average file size in bytes, average actual throughput in bytes per second, and average link load — examining whether rising grid-wide storage occupancy measurably degrades observed network transfer rates independent of file size?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) < 0.5 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) <= 0.75 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS grid_storage_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_throughput_bytes_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') != json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') GROUP BY grid_storage_tier ORDER BY CASE grid_storage_tier WHEN 'low' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For each site, what is the job count, average resource allocation duration (time from JobAllocation Started to JobAllocation Finished), maximum allocation duration, and minimum allocation duration — identifying which sites are slowest at matching incoming jobs to available resources and may become scheduling bottlenecks under heavy load?
sql: SELECT json_extract(fa.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(fa.TIME - sa.TIME) AS avg_allocation_duration, MAX(fa.TIME - sa.TIME) AS max_allocation_duration, MIN(fa.TIME - sa.TIME) AS min_allocation_duration FROM EVENTS fa JOIN EVENTS sa ON fa.JOB_ID = sa.JOB_ID AND sa.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND sa.STATE = 'Started' WHERE fa.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND fa.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_allocation_duration DESC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average resource_waiting_queue_time, average file_transfer_queue_time, average fraction of total_queue_time attributable to resource waiting, and average fraction attributable to file transfer staging — decomposing per-site queue pressure into compute-resource contention versus data-staging delays to pinpoint which bottleneck dominates at each site?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_waiting_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_transfer_time, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) ELSE NULL END) AS avg_resource_wait_fraction, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) ELSE NULL END) AS avg_file_transfer_fraction FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_resource_wait_fraction DESC

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by site, what is the count of distinct files read, total read operations, average number of reads per distinct file, average read duration, and average disk_read_bw — identifying which sites exhibit the highest file-reuse ratios, indicating where a local data cache or wider pre-placement policy would yield the greatest reduction in redundant disk I/O?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files_read, COUNT(*) AS total_read_ops, CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) / COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS avg_reads_per_file, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_reads_per_file DESC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped into cost tiers (zero: cost equals 0, low: cost below 100, medium: cost from 100 to 1000, high: cost above 1000), what is the job count, average execution duration, average flops, average retries, and count of distinct sites represented — examining whether higher-cost jobs are systematically longer, more compute-intensive, more failure-prone, and concentrated at particular sites?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) = 0 THEN 'zero' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) < 100 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL) <= 1000 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS cost_tier, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY cost_tier ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL))

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by source site, what is the transfer count, count of distinct files sent, count of distinct destination sites served, average declared bandwidth, and average link load — identifying which source sites act as the most active data hubs in the grid by distributing the widest variety of files to the largest number of downstream consumers, and whether that hub role correlates with elevated link saturation?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files_sent, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site')) AS distinct_destinations_served, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site ORDER BY distinct_files_sent DESC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average total_io_read_time, average execution duration, ratio of average total_io_read_time to average execution duration, and maximum observed total_io_read_time — identifying which sites experience the highest I/O read burden relative to their compute time, suggesting data locality problems or slow storage subsystems?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_io_read_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL)) / NULLIF(AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)), 0) AS io_read_fraction_of_duration, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_io_read_time') AS REAL)) AS max_io_read_time FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY io_read_fraction_of_duration DESC

question: For finished inter-site FileTransfer events (source_site differs from destination_site) grouped by the source-to-destination corridor, what is the transfer count, average declared bandwidth, average latency, average duration, and the ratio of average latency to average duration — revealing which inter-site corridors are most latency-dominated versus bandwidth-dominated in their observed transfer performance?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) AS avg_latency, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL)) / NULLIF(AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)), 0) AS latency_to_duration_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' AND json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') != json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') GROUP BY source_site, destination_site ORDER BY transfer_count DESC

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by site and disk, what is the read operation count, count of distinct files read, total bytes read, average read duration, and average disk_read_bw — identifying which physical disks at each site carry the heaviest read workloads and whether high-volume disks maintain consistent throughput relative to low-volume ones?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site, disk ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the total job count, count of jobs that required at least one retry, retry rate as a fraction of total jobs, average retry count across all jobs, and maximum retry count observed — identifying which sites produce the highest job failure and retry burden, suggesting instability or resource unreliability at those compute endpoints?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_jobs, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS retried_jobs, CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / COUNT(*) AS retry_rate, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS INTEGER)) AS max_retries FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY retry_rate DESC

question: For finished JobAllocation events grouped by site, what is the allocation count, average site CPU utilization at allocation completion, average site storage utilization at allocation completion, average grid CPU utilization, and maximum site storage utilization observed — revealing the resource pressure conditions under which the scheduler places jobs at each site, and whether allocations are consistently landing on already-stressed compute resources?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_site_cpu_util DESC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by whether the transfer is intra-site (source_site equals destination_site) or inter-site (source_site differs from destination_site), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average transfer duration, average declared bandwidth, and average actual throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — comparing local versus remote data movement characteristics to quantify the performance gap between intra-cluster and cross-cluster file delivery?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN json_extract(METADATA,'$.source_site') = json_extract(METADATA,'$.destination_site') THEN 'intra-site' ELSE 'inter-site' END AS transfer_scope, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_seconds, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL),0)) AS avg_actual_throughput_bps FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileTransfer' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY transfer_scope

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by retry tier (no retries: retries equal to 0, low: 1 to 2 retries, high: 3 or more retries), what is the job count, average execution duration, average total_queue_time, average resource_waiting_queue_time, average file_transfer_queue_time, and average cost — examining whether jobs requiring more retries also accumulate disproportionately large scheduling delays and higher resource expenditure?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.retries') AS INTEGER)=0 THEN 'no_retries_0' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.retries') AS INTEGER)<=2 THEN 'low_retries_1_to_2' ELSE 'high_retries_3_plus' END AS retry_tier, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_wait_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_transfer_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='JobExecution' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY retry_tier

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by file size tier (small: size below 1000000000 bytes, medium: 1000000000 to 10000000000, large: above 10000000000), what is the read count, count of distinct files, count of distinct disks, average read duration, and average actual read throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — examining whether larger files achieve higher sustained disk read rates and whether they are concentrated on specific disks?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)<1000000000 THEN 'small_below_1GB' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)<=10000000000 THEN 'medium_1GB_to_10GB' ELSE 'large_above_10GB' END AS file_size_tier, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA,'$.file')) AS distinct_files, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk')) AS distinct_disks, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)/NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.duration') AS REAL),0)) AS avg_actual_read_throughput_bps FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileRead' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY file_size_tier

question: For finished JobAllocation events grouped by site and host, what is the allocation count, count of distinct jobs allocated, average site_cpu_util at allocation completion, average site_storage_util at allocation completion, and maximum site_cpu_util observed — revealing how job placements are distributed across compute hosts within each site and whether particular hosts are consistently allocated under elevated resource pressure?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA,'$.site') AS site, json_extract(METADATA,'$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, COUNT(DISTINCT JOB_ID) AS distinct_jobs, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='JobAllocation' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY site, host ORDER BY site, allocation_count DESC

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by site, what is the write count, minimum site_storage_util observed at write completion, maximum site_storage_util, the utilization range (maximum minus minimum), count of distinct disks used, and total bytes written — revealing how much local storage occupancy fluctuates over the simulation's write activity at each site and identifying sites with the greatest storage pressure volatility?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA,'$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS write_count, MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS min_site_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_storage_util') AS REAL))-MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS util_range, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA,'$.disk')) AS distinct_disks, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA,'$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT='FileWrite' AND STATE='Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY util_range DESC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by source site, what is the transfer count, average declared bandwidth, average actual throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), average bandwidth utilization ratio (actual throughput divided by declared bandwidth), and minimum observed utilization ratio — identifying which source sites' outbound links are most efficiently exploited versus those where factors beyond raw bandwidth constrain transfer performance?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_throughput, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_bw_utilization_ratio, MIN((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS min_bw_utilization_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY source_site ORDER BY avg_bw_utilization_ratio ASC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, average actual execution duration, average theoretical minimum duration (flops divided by cores times speed), average compute efficiency ratio (theoretical divided by actual), and maximum actual duration — quantifying how close each site's jobs run to their theoretical peak compute rate and identifying sites where non-compute overhead inflates wall-clock execution time most severely?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / (CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL) * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL))) AS avg_theoretical_min_duration, AVG((CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / (CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL) * CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL))) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_compute_efficiency_ratio, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS max_actual_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_compute_efficiency_ratio DESC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped into one-hour simulation time buckets (based on the TIME column at job completion), what is the jobs-completed count, average execution duration, average cost, average grid CPU utilization, and count of distinct sites active in that bucket — revealing how job throughput, resource consumption, and grid-wide CPU pressure evolve across the simulation timeline and identifying peak-load periods?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 3600 AS INTEGER) AS simulation_hour_bucket, COUNT(*) AS jobs_completed, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites_active FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY simulation_hour_bucket ORDER BY simulation_hour_bucket

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by link load tier at transfer completion (low: link_load below 0.3, medium: 0.3 to 0.7, high: above 0.7), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average transfer duration, average actual throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average declared bandwidth — quantifying how increasing network link congestion degrades observed throughput independent of file size or distance?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) < 0.3 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL) < 0.7 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS link_load_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_throughput, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY link_load_tier ORDER BY CASE link_load_tier WHEN 'low' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site CPU utilization tier at job completion (low: site_cpu_util below 0.5, medium: 0.5 to 0.8, high: above 0.8), what is the job count, average resource waiting queue time, average file transfer queue time, average total queue time, and average execution duration — examining whether jobs finishing under high CPU pressure were also burdened with longer scheduling and data-staging delays, indicating whether CPU contention and queue congestion are correlated system-wide?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) < 0.5 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL) < 0.8 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS site_cpu_util_tier_at_finish, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.resource_waiting_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_resource_wait_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.file_transfer_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_transfer_wait_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL)) AS avg_total_queue_time, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site_cpu_util_tier_at_finish ORDER BY CASE site_cpu_util_tier_at_finish WHEN 'low' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by disk, what is the write count, count of distinct sites hosting that disk, total bytes written, average disk_write_bw in bytes per second, maximum disk_write_bw, and minimum disk_write_bw — identifying which disks exhibit the most variable write throughput across the simulation, pointing to disks shared under heavy contention or serving heterogeneous workloads?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk') AS disk, COUNT(*) AS write_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS max_disk_write_bw, MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS min_disk_write_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY disk ORDER BY write_count DESC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by host, what is the job count, total flops processed, total cost, average cost per flop (total cost divided by total flops), average execution duration, and count of distinct sites that assigned work to that host — revealing which compute hosts deliver the most cost-effective flop throughput and whether hosts shared across multiple sites are systematically more or less economical?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS job_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS total_flops, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS total_cost, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) / NULLIF(SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)), 0) AS avg_cost_per_flop, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_execution_duration, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host ORDER BY job_count DESC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by file, what is the count of distinct destination sites the file was delivered to, total transfer count, total bytes transferred across all deliveries, average transfer duration, and average link load — identifying which files are most broadly distributed across the grid and whether widely-replicated files experience systematically higher link contention during delivery?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site')) AS distinct_destinations, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_transferred, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file ORDER BY distinct_destinations DESC

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped into one-hour simulation time buckets (based on the TIME column at write completion), what is the write count, average grid_storage_util, maximum grid_storage_util, total bytes written, and count of distinct sites performing writes — revealing how grid-wide storage occupancy and write activity evolve across the simulation timeline and pinpointing periods of peak storage pressure?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 3600 AS INTEGER) AS hour_bucket, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS max_grid_storage_util, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_written, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY hour_bucket ORDER BY hour_bucket

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the total job count, count of jobs with zero total_queue_time, count of jobs with positive total_queue_time, average execution duration for zero-queue-time jobs, and average execution duration for positive-queue-time jobs — revealing whether prior scheduling delays correlate with longer or shorter actual execution at each site and identifying sites where jobs systematically bypass the queue?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS total_jobs, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS zero_queue_jobs, SUM(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS positive_queue_jobs, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) = 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) END) AS avg_duration_zero_queue, AVG(CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.total_queue_time') AS REAL) > 0 THEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL) END) AS avg_duration_positive_queue FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY total_jobs DESC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by the number of cores used, what is the job count, average execution duration, average flops per core (flops divided by cores), average cost, and count of distinct sites — examining whether higher core-count jobs achieve proportionally greater per-core flop throughput and whether core count correlates with cost efficiency across the grid?
sql: SELECT CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS INTEGER) AS cores, COUNT(*) AS job_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cores') AS REAL)) AS avg_flops_per_core, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY cores ORDER BY cores

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by network latency tier (low: latency below 0.001, medium: 0.001 to 0.01, high: above 0.01), what is the transfer count, average declared bandwidth, average transfer duration, average actual throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average link load — quantifying how increasing inter-site latency degrades observed transfer performance independently of declared link capacity?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) < 0.001 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL) <= 0.01 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS latency_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_throughput_bytes_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY latency_tier ORDER BY AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.latency') AS REAL))

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by host, what is the read operation count, count of distinct files read, count of distinct sites that host appears in, total bytes read, average read duration, and average disk_read_bw — identifying which compute hosts are the most intensive consumers of disk I/O across the simulation and whether hosts that read the most data maintain consistent throughput?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host') AS host, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY host ORDER BY total_bytes_read DESC

question: For finished FileWrite events grouped by grid storage utilization tier at write completion (low: grid_storage_util below 0.5, medium: 0.5 to 0.75, high: above 0.75), what is the write count, average disk_write_bw, average write duration, total bytes written, and count of distinct sites performing writes — examining whether rising global storage occupancy measurably suppresses disk write throughput across the entire grid?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) < 0.5 THEN 'low' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL) <= 0.75 THEN 'medium' ELSE 'high' END AS grid_storage_util_tier, COUNT(*) AS write_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_write_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_write_bw, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_write_duration_sec, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS INTEGER)) AS total_bytes_written, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY grid_storage_util_tier ORDER BY MIN(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL))

question: For finished JobAllocation events grouped by one-hour simulation time bucket (based on the TIME column at allocation completion), what is the allocation count, count of distinct jobs allocated, count of distinct sites receiving allocations, average site_cpu_util, and average grid_storage_util — revealing how scheduling throughput and site-level resource pressure co-evolve across the simulation timeline and pinpointing periods of peak allocation demand?
sql: SELECT CAST(TIME / 3600 AS INTEGER) AS hour_bucket, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, COUNT(DISTINCT JOB_ID) AS distinct_jobs, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY hour_bucket ORDER BY hour_bucket

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by destination site, what is the transfer count, count of distinct source sites feeding data in, count of distinct files received, total bytes received, and average actual throughput in bytes per second (file size divided by transfer duration) — identifying which destination sites are the heaviest data consumers in the grid and whether sites that receive from many source sites achieve systematically better or worse per-transfer throughput?
sql:
SELECT
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site') AS destination_site,
    COUNT(*) AS transfer_count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site')) AS distinct_source_sites,
    COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file')) AS distinct_files_received,
    SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_received,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_throughput_bps
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY destination_site
ORDER BY total_bytes_received DESC

question: For each site, aggregating finished FileRead and FileWrite events together, what is the total read operation count, total write operation count, total bytes read, total bytes written, and the read-to-write byte ratio — revealing which sites are net data producers versus consumers and identifying sites with the most skewed versus balanced disk I/O workloads across the simulation?
sql:
SELECT
    site,
    SUM(read_ops) AS total_read_ops,
    SUM(write_ops) AS total_write_ops,
    SUM(bytes_read) AS total_bytes_read,
    SUM(bytes_written) AS total_bytes_written,
    CASE WHEN SUM(bytes_written) > 0
         THEN CAST(SUM(bytes_read) AS REAL) / SUM(bytes_written)
         ELSE NULL END AS read_to_write_byte_ratio
FROM (
    SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site,
           COUNT(*) AS read_ops, 0 AS write_ops,
           SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS bytes_read,
           0.0 AS bytes_written
    FROM EVENTS
    WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished'
    GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')
    UNION ALL
    SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site,
           0 AS read_ops, COUNT(*) AS write_ops,
           0.0 AS bytes_read,
           SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS bytes_written
    FROM EVENTS
    WHERE EVENT = 'FileWrite' AND STATE = 'Finished'
    GROUP BY json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')
) combined
GROUP BY site
ORDER BY read_to_write_byte_ratio DESC

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped into file size tiers (small: below 1000000000 bytes, medium: 1000000000 to 10000000000, large: above 10000000000), what is the transfer count, count of distinct source sites, count of distinct destination sites, average transfer duration, average declared bandwidth, and average actual throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration) — examining whether larger files achieve higher sustained network throughput or instead disproportionately saturate inter-site links relative to smaller transfers?
sql:
SELECT
    CASE
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'small'
        WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) <= 10000000000 THEN 'medium'
        ELSE 'large'
    END AS size_tier,
    COUNT(*) AS transfer_count,
    COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site')) AS distinct_source_sites,
    COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.destination_site')) AS distinct_destination_sites,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_actual_throughput_bps
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY size_tier
ORDER BY CASE size_tier WHEN 'small' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For FileTransfer Started events grouped by source site, what is the count of outbound transfer initiations, average site_storage_util, average grid_storage_util, average link_load, and average declared bandwidth at the moment each transfer begins — measuring the typical storage occupancy and network congestion conditions under which each site launches outbound data movement, and revealing whether sites with higher average link loads also exhibit elevated storage pressure at dispatch time?
sql:
SELECT
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.source_site') AS source_site,
    COUNT(*) AS transfer_start_count,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_storage_util_at_start,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_storage_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_storage_util_at_start,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.link_load') AS REAL)) AS avg_link_load_at_start,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL)) AS avg_declared_bandwidth
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Started'
GROUP BY source_site
ORDER BY avg_link_load_at_start DESC

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by job STATUS value and site, what is the job count, average execution duration, average retries, average cost, and count of distinct hosts used — revealing whether particular completion status values are concentrated at specific sites and whether non-standard statuses correlate with longer runtimes, higher retry burdens, or elevated resource expenditure compared to the dominant status at that site?
sql:
SELECT
    STATUS,
    json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site,
    COUNT(*) AS job_count,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_duration,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.retries') AS REAL)) AS avg_retries,
    AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS avg_cost,
    COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts
FROM EVENTS
WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished'
GROUP BY STATUS, site
ORDER BY STATUS, avg_retries DESC

question: For each site, what is the job allocation count, average allocation duration in seconds (computed as the difference between each JobAllocation Finished TIME and its corresponding Started TIME for the same JOB_ID), maximum allocation duration, minimum allocation duration, and average grid CPU utilization at allocation completion — revealing which sites impose the longest scheduling delays before resources are committed to waiting jobs and whether grid-wide CPU pressure correlates with slower allocation processing?
sql: SELECT json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS allocation_count, AVG(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS avg_allocation_duration_sec, MAX(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS max_allocation_duration_sec, MIN(f.TIME - s.TIME) AS min_allocation_duration_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(f.METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util_at_finish FROM EVENTS f JOIN EVENTS s ON s.JOB_ID = f.JOB_ID AND s.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND s.STATE = 'Started' WHERE f.EVENT = 'JobAllocation' AND f.STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY avg_allocation_duration_sec DESC

question: For finished FileRead events grouped by file, what is the read count, count of distinct sites that accessed the file, count of distinct hosts that read it, total bytes read, average read duration, and average disk_read_bw — identifying which specific data objects are the most broadly and frequently accessed across the grid, and whether high-demand files maintain consistent read throughput or suffer measurable degradation under repeated access pressure?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.file') AS file, COUNT(*) AS read_count, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site')) AS distinct_sites, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS total_bytes_read, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_read_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.disk_read_bw') AS REAL)) AS avg_disk_read_bw FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileRead' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY file ORDER BY read_count DESC

question: For JobExecution events grouped by site and STATE (Started versus Finished), what is the event count, average site CPU utilization, average grid CPU utilization, and maximum site CPU utilization — revealing whether per-site CPU pressure systematically rises or falls between the moment a job begins executing and the moment it completes, indicating whether jobs are landing on increasingly loaded or progressively drained resources over their lifetimes?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, STATE, COUNT(*) AS event_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_site_cpu_util, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.grid_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS avg_grid_cpu_util, MAX(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.site_cpu_util') AS REAL)) AS max_site_cpu_util FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' GROUP BY site, STATE ORDER BY site, STATE

question: For finished FileTransfer events grouped by declared bandwidth tier (narrow: below 1000000000 bytes per second, medium: 1000000000 to 10000000000, wide: above 10000000000), what is the transfer count, average file size in bytes, average transfer duration, average actual throughput in bytes per second (size divided by duration), and average bandwidth utilization ratio (actual throughput divided by declared bandwidth) — examining whether higher-capacity links are proportionally better exploited or whether diminishing utilization emerges at progressively wider link tiers?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) < 1000000000 THEN 'narrow_below_1e9' WHEN CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL) <= 10000000000 THEN 'medium_1e9_to_1e10' ELSE 'wide_above_1e10' END AS bandwidth_tier, COUNT(*) AS transfer_count, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL)) AS avg_file_size_bytes, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL)) AS avg_transfer_duration, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_actual_throughput_bytes_per_sec, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.size') AS REAL) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.duration') AS REAL), 0) / NULLIF(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.bandwidth') AS REAL), 0)) AS avg_bandwidth_utilization_ratio FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'FileTransfer' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY bandwidth_tier ORDER BY CASE bandwidth_tier WHEN 'narrow_below_1e9' THEN 1 WHEN 'medium_1e9_to_1e10' THEN 2 ELSE 3 END

question: For finished JobExecution events grouped by site, what is the job count, total cost, total flops processed, cost per flop (total cost divided by total flops), average declared host speed, and count of distinct hosts used — revealing which sites deliver the most cost-effective compute throughput and how unit compute expenditure varies across the grid, identifying whether higher-speed sites translate their hardware advantage into proportionally lower per-flop cost?
sql: SELECT json_extract(METADATA, '$.site') AS site, COUNT(*) AS job_count, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) AS total_cost, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)) AS total_flops, SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.cost') AS REAL)) / NULLIF(SUM(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.flops') AS REAL)), 0) AS cost_per_flop, AVG(CAST(json_extract(METADATA, '$.speed') AS REAL)) AS avg_host_speed, COUNT(DISTINCT json_extract(METADATA, '$.host')) AS distinct_hosts FROM EVENTS WHERE EVENT = 'JobExecution' AND STATE = 'Finished' GROUP BY site ORDER BY cost_per_flop
